symbols.name: _get_version
symbols.docstring:
Get the current version of Exportify.

Because our version is dynamically generated during build/release, we try several methods to get it. If you downloaded Exportify from PyPi, then the first will work, or the second if the file didn't get generated for some reason. If you're running from source, we try to get the version from git tags. If all else fails, we return "0.0.0".

symbols.name: __dir__
symbols.docstring: List available attributes for the package.

symbols.name: __dir__
symbols.docstring: List available attributes for the package.

symbols.name: ASTParser
symbols.docstring: Parse Python files and extract exports.

symbols.name: is_overloaded_function
symbols.docstring:
Check if function has @overload decorator.

Args:
    node: Function definition AST node

Returns:
    True if function has @overload decorator

Example:
    >>> node = ast.parse("@overload\ndef f(): ...").body[0]
    >>> is_overloaded_function(node)
    True

symbols.name: group_functions_by_name
symbols.docstring:
Group top-level functions by name, handling @overload.

Args:
    tree: Parsed AST module

Returns:
    Dict mapping function name to metadata about all definitions

Example:
    >>> tree = ast.parse('''
    ... @overload
    ... def f(x: int) -> int: ...
    ... @overload
    ... def f(x: str) -> str: ...
    ... def f(x): return x
    ... ''')
    >>> groups = group_functions_by_name(tree)
    >>> groups["f"]["has_overload"]
    True
    >>> groups["f"]["overload_count"]
    2

symbols.name: main
symbols.docstring: Main entry point for the CLI.

symbols.name: __dir__
symbols.docstring: List available attributes for the package.

symbols.name: _resolve_checks
symbols.docstring:
Resolve which checks to run given bool|None flags.

Logic:
- If ANY flag is True (explicitly given) → whitelist mode: only run those checks.
- If ONLY False flags (--no-X) → blacklist mode: run everything except those.
- If all None (no flags given) → run all checks.

symbols.name: _display_all_modifications
symbols.docstring: Helper to display __all__ modifications in a human-friendly way.

symbols.name: _run_lateimports_check
symbols.docstring: Run the lateimports check and return (errors, warnings).

symbols.name: _run_dynamic_imports_check
symbols.docstring: Run the dynamic_imports check and return (errors, warnings).

symbols.name: _run_module_all_check
symbols.docstring: Run the module_all check and return (errors, warnings).

symbols.name: _run_package_all_check
symbols.docstring: Run the package_all check and return (errors, warnings).

symbols.name: _print_final_status
symbols.docstring: Print final check status and exit if needed.

symbols.name: check
symbols.docstring:
Validate exports and __all__ declarations for consistency.

Checks:
- lateimport() / LateImport calls resolve to real modules (--lateimports)
- _dynamic_imports entries in __init__.py files resolve correctly and match __all__ (--dynamic-imports)
- __all__ in regular modules matches configured export rules (--module-all)
- __all__ and exports in __init__.py files are consistent with each other (--package-all)

Pass one or more flags explicitly to run only those checks.
Use --no-X flags to skip specific checks while running the rest.
Omit all flags to run every check.

Note: The lateimports check is automatically skipped if 'lateimport' is not
listed as a project dependency (it's an opt-in library).

Examples:
    exportify check
    exportify check --module-all
    exportify check --no-lateimports
    exportify check --strict
    exportify check src/mypackage/core
    exportify check --json

symbols.name: clear_cache
symbols.docstring:
Delete all cached analysis results.

Removes every entry from the analysis cache. Exportify rebuilds
the cache automatically on the next ``validate`` or ``generate`` run.

Use this when:
- The cache is corrupted
- You upgrade Exportify and the cache schema changes
- Stale cache entries cause unexpected behavior

Examples:
    exportify clear-cache

symbols.name: doctor
symbols.docstring:
Check Exportify cache and configuration health.

Checks:
- Cache entries (total, valid, invalid, size, hit rate)
- Rule configuration (config file present or missing)

Warns if more than 10% of cache entries are invalid and
recommends running ``exportify clear-cache``.

Examples:
    exportify doctor

symbols.name: _resolve_fix_checks
symbols.docstring:
Resolve which fix operations to run given bool|None flags.

Same logic as _resolve_checks but for the fix command (no lateimports).

symbols.name: _report_module_all_dry_run
symbols.docstring: Print dry-run diff for a single module __all__ fix.

symbols.name: _report_module_all_applied
symbols.docstring: Print applied-fix info for a single module __all__ fix.

symbols.name: _fix_module_all_files
symbols.docstring: Fix __all__ in regular (non-__init__.py) modules. Returns count of changed files.

symbols.name: _warn_missing_inits
symbols.docstring: Warn about package directories that are missing __init__.py.

symbols.name: _fix_init_files
symbols.docstring: Fix __init__.py files via the Pipeline. Returns count of changed files.

symbols.name: _print_summary
symbols.docstring: Print the final summary line.

symbols.name: fix
symbols.docstring:
Sync exports and __all__ declarations to match configured rules.

Updates:
- __all__ in regular modules to match export rules (--module-all)
- _dynamic_imports and __all__ in __init__.py files (--dynamic-imports, --package-all)

Skips lateimport() call paths — those import targets require manual correction.

Use --dry-run to preview all changes before writing any files.

Warns when a package directory has no __init__.py and suggests running `generate` first.

Examples:
    exportify fix
    exportify fix --dry-run
    exportify fix --module-all
    exportify fix src/mypackage/core

symbols.name: _print_generation_results
symbols.docstring: Print export generation results with colors.

symbols.name: generate
symbols.docstring:
Create and update __init__.py files with lazy exports across your package.

Runs the full export pipeline: discovers Python files, applies export rules,
then writes or updates every __init__.py in the source tree. New packages get
fresh files; existing ones are updated in-place, preserving any code above the
managed-exports sentinel.

Each generated file includes:
- A __all__ declaration listing public exports
- lateimport() calls for lazy loading (or barrel imports if configured)
- TYPE_CHECKING imports where appropriate

Use `fix` to make targeted updates — syncing __all__ and _dynamic_imports
without re-running the full pipeline.

Examples:
    exportify generate
    exportify generate --dry-run
    exportify generate --module src/mypackage/core
    exportify generate --source src/mypackage --output /tmp/test

symbols.name: init
symbols.docstring:
Initialize exportify with a default configuration file.

Creates `.exportify.yaml` in the current directory with reasonable default
rules that work for most Python packages. Edit the file afterwards to
customize which symbols are exported and how they propagate.

Examples:
    exportify init
    exportify init --dry-run
    exportify init --output path/to/rules.yaml
    exportify init --force
    exportify init --verbose

symbols.name: status
symbols.docstring:
Show a quick snapshot of Exportify's current state.

Displays:
- Cache status (valid/total entries, hit rate)
- Configuration status (rules file found or missing)
- System readiness

Pass ``--verbose`` to also show cache size and invalid entry count.

Examples:
    exportify status
    exportify status --verbose

symbols.name: undo
symbols.docstring:
Restore files modified by the last fix run.

Reads the snapshot taken before the most recent ``fix`` run and
restores the original content.  Idempotent — safe to run multiple times.

If paths are given, only matching files are restored.

Examples:
    exportify undo
    exportify undo src/mypackage/
    exportify undo src/foo/__init__.py src/bar/__init__.py

symbols.name: print_validation_results

symbols.name: print_output_validation_json

symbols.name: print_output_validation_verbose

symbols.name: print_output_validation_concise

symbols.name: load_rules
symbols.docstring: Load rules from config file, falling back to defaults.

symbols.name: collect_py_files
symbols.docstring:
Collect Python files from given paths or auto-detect source root.

Args:
    paths: Explicit paths to check. If empty, auto-detect source root.
    source: Optional source root override.

Returns:
    List of Python file paths to process.

symbols.name: path_to_module
symbols.docstring: Convert a file path to a module path.

symbols.name: __dir__
symbols.docstring: List available attributes for the package.

symbols.name: _add_ignore_to_cache
symbols.docstring: Add .gitignore entry for cache file.

symbols.name: CircuitState
symbols.docstring: State of the circuit breaker.

symbols.name: CircuitBreaker
symbols.docstring:
Circuit breaker pattern for cache failure handling.

Prevents cascading failures by opening the circuit after repeated failures,
then periodically testing if the service has recovered.

symbols.name: JSONAnalysisCache
symbols.docstring:
JSON-based analysis result cache with schema versioning.

Provides persistent caching of AST analysis results with:
- Schema versioning and migration support
- SHA-256 file hashing for cache validation
- Circuit breaker pattern for resilience
- Automatic cache invalidation on file changes
- Persistent storage with JSON serialization

symbols.name: find_config_file
symbols.docstring:
Find the exportify config file.

Checks, in order:

1. ``EXPORTIFY_CONFIG`` environment variable — any valid path is accepted.
2. ``.exportify/config.yaml`` / ``.exportify/config.yml`` in the current working directory.
3. ``.exportify.yaml`` / ``.exportify.yml`` in the current working directory.
4. ``exportify.yaml`` / ``exportify.yml`` in the current working directory.

Returns:
    Path to the config file, or ``None`` if none is found.

symbols.name: SpdxConfig
symbols.docstring: SPDX license header configuration for generated __init__.py files.

symbols.name: ExportifyConfig
symbols.docstring: Parsed exportify configuration.

symbols.name: load_config
symbols.docstring:
Load an exportify configuration from a YAML file.

Reads the file at *path* and populates an :class:`ExportifyConfig`.  Only
the ``output_style`` and ``overrides`` sections are relevant to this
function; all other YAML keys (e.g. ``schema_version``, ``rules``) are
silently ignored.

YAML schema understood by this function::

    output_style: lazy  # or "barrel"; default "lazy"

    overrides:
      "mypackage.compat":
        output_style: barrel

Args:
    path: Path to the YAML config file.

Returns:
    A populated :class:`ExportifyConfig`.

Raises:
    ValueError: If ``output_style`` (top-level or in an override) contains
        an unrecognized value.

symbols.name: detect_lateimport_dependency
symbols.docstring:
Return True if 'lateimport' is listed as a project dependency in pyproject.toml.

Checks both ``project.dependencies`` (runtime) and every list under
``dependency-groups`` (e.g. dev dependencies added via ``uv add --dev``).

``pyproject.toml`` is looked up relative to the current working directory
(``Path.cwd() / "pyproject.toml"``), consistent with the behavior of
:func:`find_config_file`.

Returns:
    ``True`` if any dependency entry starting with ``"lateimport"`` is
    found; ``False`` otherwise (including when ``pyproject.toml`` is absent
    or cannot be parsed).

symbols.name: SnapshotEntry
symbols.docstring: One file in the snapshot.

symbols.name: SnapshotManifest
symbols.docstring: Full manifest for a snapshot.

symbols.name: SnapshotManager
symbols.docstring:
Manages pre-flight snapshots for exportify fix runs.

Snapshots are stored in .exportify/snapshots/last/ relative to the
project root. Only one snapshot (the most recent) is kept.

symbols.name: OutputStyle
symbols.docstring: Output style for generated __init__.py files.

symbols.name: PropagationLevel

symbols.name: RuleAction

symbols.name: SourceLocation

symbols.name: DetectedSymbol

symbols.name: ExportDecision

symbols.name: LazyExport
symbols.docstring: A finalized export entry for __init__.py generation (Graph Phase).

symbols.name: ExportManifest
symbols.docstring:
Export manifest for a single module.

Contains all exports for a module: both its own exports and those
propagated from child modules.

symbols.name: RuleMatchCriteria
symbols.docstring: Criteria for matching exports.

symbols.name: Rule
symbols.docstring:
A rule for export decisions.

Rules are evaluated in priority order (higher = first).
When multiple rules match, the highest priority wins.
For same priority, alphabetically first rule name wins.

symbols.name: RuleMatch
symbols.docstring: A rule that matched an export.

symbols.name: RuleEvaluationResult
symbols.docstring: Result of evaluating rules for an export.

symbols.name: GeneratedFile

symbols.name: UpdatedFile

symbols.name: SkippedFile

symbols.name: GenerationMetrics
symbols.docstring: Metrics from export generation.

symbols.name: ExportGenerationResult
symbols.docstring: Complete result of export generation process.

symbols.name: ValidationError
symbols.docstring: A validation error that must be fixed.

symbols.name: ValidationWarning
symbols.docstring: A validation warning (non-critical).

symbols.name: ValidationMetrics
symbols.docstring: Metrics from validation.

symbols.name: ValidationReport
symbols.docstring: Complete validation result.

symbols.name: AnalysisResult
symbols.docstring: Cached analysis of a Python file.

symbols.name: CacheEntry

symbols.name: CacheStatistics

symbols.name: CoordinatedResult

symbols.name: LateImportConfig

symbols.name: ValidationConfig

symbols.name: ImportResolution

symbols.name: ConsistencyIssue

symbols.name: CallError

symbols.name: __dir__
symbols.docstring: List available attributes for the package.

symbols.name: FileDiscovery
symbols.docstring: Discover Python files in source tree.

symbols.name: __dir__
symbols.docstring: List available attributes for the package.

symbols.name: WriteResult
symbols.docstring: Result of a file write operation.

symbols.name: FileWriter
symbols.docstring:
Handles atomic file writes with validation.

Writes are performed via a temp file + atomic rename, so the target
file is never left in a partial state.  Pre-run snapshots (see
SnapshotManager) provide user-facing rollback instead of per-file backups.

symbols.name: _export_sort_key
symbols.docstring:
Sort key for exports: SCREAMING_SNAKE (0), PascalCase (1), snake_case (2).

Args:
    name: Export name to classify

Returns:
    Tuple of (group_number, lowercase_name) for sorting

symbols.name: GeneratedCode
symbols.docstring: Generated code for a module's __init__.py file.

symbols.name: CodeGenerator
symbols.docstring: Generates __init__.py files from export manifests.

symbols.name: _check_ast_declarations
symbols.docstring: Check for __all__ in AST.

symbols.name: _validate_sentinel_section
symbols.docstring: Validate sentinel and managed section.

symbols.name: validate_init_file
symbols.docstring: Validate an existing __init__.py file.

symbols.name: CircularDependencyIndicator
symbols.docstring: Indicator for circular dependencies in export propagation.

symbols.name: ExportEntry
symbols.docstring: Internal graph representation of an export.

symbols.name: ModuleNode
symbols.docstring: A node in the module hierarchy graph.

symbols.name: export_sort_key
symbols.docstring: Sort key: SCREAMING_SNAKE (0), PascalCase (1), snake_case (2).

symbols.name: PropagationGraph
symbols.docstring: Manages export propagation through module hierarchy.

symbols.name: _export_sort_key
symbols.docstring:
Sort key: SCREAMING_SNAKE (0), PascalCase (1), snake_case (2).

Args:
    name: Export name to classify.

Returns:
    Tuple of (group_number, lowercase_name) for stable sorting.

symbols.name: ModuleAllIssue
symbols.docstring:
An issue found with ``__all__`` in a regular module.

Attributes:
    file: Path to the Python source file.
    issue_type: One of ``"missing"``, ``"extra"``, or ``"no_all"``.
    symbol_name: The symbol name involved in the issue.
    message: Human-readable description of the issue.

symbols.name: ModuleAllFixResult
symbols.docstring:
Result of fixing ``__all__`` in a regular module.

Attributes:
    file: Path to the Python source file.
    was_modified: Whether the file was (or would be) modified.
    dry_run: If ``True`` the file was not written to disk.
    added: Names added (or that would be added) to ``__all__``.
    removed: Names removed (or that would be removed) from ``__all__``.
    created: ``True`` when ``__all__`` was created from scratch because it
        did not exist before.

symbols.name: _compute_rule_actions
symbols.docstring:
Return a mapping of symbol name to RuleAction for all DEFINED_HERE symbols.

Only ``DEFINED_HERE`` symbols are considered; imports are intentionally
excluded.

Args:
    file: Path to the Python source file.
    module_path: Dotted module path (e.g. ``"mypackage.core.utils"``).
    rules: Configured :class:`~exportify.export_manager.rules.RuleEngine`.
    tree: Optional already-parsed AST module.  Unused by the current
        implementation but accepted so callers can signal intent; the
        ASTParser re-reads the file internally via its own path-based API.

Returns:
    Mapping of symbol name to its :class:`~exportify.common.types.RuleAction`.

symbols.name: _find_all_node
symbols.docstring:
Find the module-level ``__all__ = ...`` assignment node, if present.

Only the *first* top-level assignment whose sole target is ``__all__`` is
returned.  Augmented assignments (``__all__ += [...]``) are ignored.

Args:
    tree: Parsed AST of the module.

Returns:
    The :class:`ast.Assign` node or ``None``.

symbols.name: _extract_all_names
symbols.docstring:
Extract the string constants from an ``__all__`` assignment node.

Supports both list (``[...]``) and tuple (``(...)``) literal values.
Non-string elements are silently ignored.

Args:
    node: The ``__all__`` assignment node.

Returns:
    Ordered list of names in ``__all__``.

symbols.name: _detect_all_format
symbols.docstring:
Return whether the existing ``__all__`` uses a list or tuple literal.

Args:
    node: The ``__all__`` assignment node.

Returns:
    ``"list"`` or ``"tuple"``.

symbols.name: _render_all
symbols.docstring:
Render an ``__all__`` assignment as source text.

Args:
    names: Names to include, already in the desired order.
    fmt: Whether to render as a ``list`` or ``tuple``.

Returns:
    Source text for the complete ``__all__ = ...`` assignment.

symbols.name: _merge_names
symbols.docstring:
Compute the merged ``__all__`` name list together with diff information.

The merge strategy:
- Names present in *existing* that are in *should_export* or have
  NO_DECISION (i.e., not in *should_exclude*) are kept in their original
  relative order.
- New names (``should_export - existing``) are appended in sorted order
  (using :func:`_export_sort_key`).
- Names in *existing* that are in *should_exclude* are removed.
  Names with NO_DECISION that are already in ``__all__`` are left alone.

Args:
    existing: Current, ordered list of names in ``__all__``.
    should_export: Set of names with ``INCLUDE`` action from the rule engine.
    should_exclude: Set of names with ``EXCLUDE`` action from the rule engine.

Returns:
    Three-tuple of ``(merged_names, added_names, removed_names)``.

symbols.name: _node_line_range
symbols.docstring:
Return the inclusive 0-based line range occupied by *node* in the source.

``ast.Assign`` gives us ``node.lineno`` (1-based start) but the ``end_lineno``
attribute (Python 3.8+) is the most reliable way to determine where the
statement ends.

Args:
    source_lines: Source split into lines (no newline terminators needed).
    node: The AST assignment node.

Returns:
    ``(start_idx, end_idx)`` where both are 0-based indices into
    *source_lines*, inclusive.

symbols.name: check_module_all
symbols.docstring:
Check that ``__all__`` in a regular module matches what the rules prescribe.

Returns a list of :class:`ModuleAllIssue` objects:

- ``"missing"`` — a name the rules say should be exported (``INCLUDE``) is
  absent from ``__all__``.
- ``"extra"`` — a name in ``__all__`` that the rules explicitly say should
  *not* be exported (``EXCLUDE``).  Names with ``NO_DECISION`` that are
  already in ``__all__`` are **not** flagged.
- ``"no_all"`` — ``__all__`` is absent entirely but the rules prescribe at
  least one export.  A single issue with an empty ``symbol_name`` is returned.

If neither ``__all__`` is present nor any exports are prescribed, an empty
list is returned (nothing to do).

Args:
    file: Path to the Python source file.
    module_path: Dotted module path (e.g. ``"mypackage.core.utils"``).
    rules: Configured :class:`~exportify.export_manager.rules.RuleEngine`.

Returns:
    Possibly-empty list of :class:`ModuleAllIssue` instances.

symbols.name: fix_module_all
symbols.docstring:
Update ``__all__`` in a regular module to match what the rules prescribe.

Behavior:

- If ``__all__`` already exists: names with ``EXCLUDE`` action are removed,
  names with ``INCLUDE`` action that are missing are appended (sorted), and
  names with ``NO_DECISION`` that are already present are left untouched.
  The existing ordering of retained names is preserved.
- If ``__all__`` is absent: it is created at the end of the file (preceded
  by a blank line) as a tuple literal, sorted by :func:`_export_sort_key`.
- If no exports are prescribed **and** ``__all__`` is absent, the file is
  left unchanged (``was_modified=False``).
- When *dry_run* is ``True`` the function computes and returns what *would*
  change but does not write to disk.

Args:
    file: Path to the Python source file.
    module_path: Dotted module path (e.g. ``"mypackage.core.utils"``).
    rules: Configured :class:`~exportify.export_manager.rules.RuleEngine`.
    dry_run: When ``True``, return the result without modifying the file.

Returns:
    A :class:`ModuleAllFixResult` describing what was (or would be) done.

symbols.name: _generate_all_from_exports
symbols.docstring: Generate a new __all__ declaration from the prescribed exports when __all__ is absent.

symbols.name: _append_all
symbols.docstring:
Append a new ``__all__`` declaration to the end of *source*.

A blank separator line is inserted between the existing content and the new
declaration.  A trailing newline is ensured at the very end.

Args:
    source: Current file source text.
    new_all_text: The rendered ``__all__ = ...`` source text.

Returns:
    Updated source text.

symbols.name: SchemaVersionError
symbols.docstring: Schema version mismatch or unsupported.

symbols.name: RuleEngine
symbols.docstring:
Registry and evaluator for all rules.

The rule engine implements a priority-based system for deciding whether
exports should be included and how far they should propagate. Rules are
evaluated in priority order (highest first), and the first matching rule
determines the action.

symbols.name: _PreservedData
symbols.docstring: Internal type for preserved code extraction.

symbols.name: ParsedSections
symbols.docstring:
Result of parsing an existing __init__.py file.

Attributes:
    preserved_code: User code to preserve (formatted, ready for injection)
    had_type_checking: Whether file had a TYPE_CHECKING block
    had_dynamic_imports: Whether file had _dynamic_imports assignment
    had_getattr: Whether file had __getattr__ assignment
    had_all: Whether file had __all__ assignment
    had_dir: Whether file had __dir__() function
    docstring: Module docstring if present
    managed_lines: Line numbers of managed sections for debugging
    preserved_lines: Line numbers of preserved sections for debugging

symbols.name: SectionParser
symbols.docstring: AST-based parser for identifying managed vs preserved code sections.

symbols.name: parse_init_file
symbols.docstring:
Parse an existing __init__.py file (convenience function).

Args:
    file_path: Path to __init__.py file

Returns:
    ParsedSections with preserved code and metadata

symbols.name: ExtractedRule
symbols.docstring: A rule that will appear in the generated configuration.

symbols.name: MigrationResult
symbols.docstring: Result of the configuration-generation process.

symbols.name: RuleMigrator
symbols.docstring: Generates a default exportify YAML configuration.

symbols.name: _rule_from_yaml
symbols.docstring:
Parse a flat YAML rule dict into an ExtractedRule.

For rules with complex ``any_of``/``all_of`` match blocks, only the
top-level match fields (``name_pattern``, ``name_exact``, ``member_type``)
are captured — this is sufficient for reporting purposes.

symbols.name: _load_init_template
symbols.docstring: Read the bundled init template and parse it into ExtractedRule objects.

symbols.name: _generate_template_summary
symbols.docstring: Return a human-readable summary of the template rules.

symbols.name: migrate_to_yaml
symbols.docstring:
Generate default exportify configuration and optionally write it.

Loads the bundled ``init_template.yaml`` — the full default rule set with
correct propagation settings — and writes it to ``output_path``.

Args:
    output_path: Destination for the YAML config file.
    dry_run: If True, generate but do not write any files.

Returns:
    MigrationResult with YAML content and generation details.

symbols.name: _validate_private_member
symbols.docstring: Validate private member exclusion.

symbols.name: _validate_constant
symbols.docstring: Validate constant inclusion.

symbols.name: _validate_exception_class
symbols.docstring: Validate exception class inclusion and propagation.

symbols.name: verify_migration
symbols.docstring:
Verify that a generated config produces the expected behavior.

Args:
    yaml_path: Path to the generated YAML config.
    test_cases: Tuples of (symbol_name, module_path, member_type) to validate.
        Defaults to a standard set covering common symbol patterns.

Returns:
    (success, errors) tuple.

symbols.name: cli_init
symbols.docstring:
CLI helper for the init command.

Args:
    output: Output path for the YAML config.
    dry_run: If True, show output but don't write.
    verbose: If True, show the full configuration summary.

Returns:
    Exit code (0 = success, 1 = failure).

symbols.name: detect_source_root
symbols.docstring:
Detect the Python source root for the current project.

Resolution order:
1. ``pyproject.toml`` -- common build-backend conventions (Hatch, Setuptools, Flit)
2. ``src/`` directory exists → return ``Path("src")``
3. Fall back to ``Path(".")`` (project root / flat layout)

symbols.name: detect_lateimport_dependency
symbols.docstring: Detect if the project has a dependency on late-import.

symbols.docstring: Check if ruff is installed and available.

symbols.name: _has_isort
symbols.docstring: Check if isort is installed and available.

symbols.name: _has_black
symbols.docstring: Check if black is installed and available.

symbols.name: formatting_tools_available
symbols.docstring: Check if any formatting tools are available.

symbols.name: format_content
symbols.docstring:
Format Python source code using the first available formatter via stdin.

Pipes ``content`` to the formatter's stdin and returns the formatted
result.  Passing ``filename`` lets the formatter discover project config
(``ruff.toml``, ``pyproject.toml``, etc.) without the file needing to
exist on disk.

Falls back to returning ``content`` unchanged when no formatter is
installed or when the formatter exits non-zero.

Args:
    content: Python source code to format.
    filename: Optional path hint forwarded as ``--stdin-filename``.
    verbose: Whether to show formatter output.

Returns:
    Formatted source code, or the original ``content`` if formatting
    is unavailable or fails.

symbols.name: format_file
symbols.docstring:
Format a file in-place using the first available formatter tool.

Reads the file, pipes content through :func:`format_content`, and
writes the result back only when the content changes.  Silently does
nothing if no formatter is installed.

Args:
    file_path: Path to the Python file to format.
    verbose: Whether to show formatter output.

symbols.name: __dir__
symbols.docstring: List available attributes for the package.

symbols.name: ConsistencyChecker
symbols.docstring:
Checks consistency between __init__.py and source files.

Validates that:
- __all__ declarations match _dynamic_imports
- _dynamic_imports entries exist in source modules
- TYPE_CHECKING blocks are properly structured

symbols.name: ImportResolver
symbols.docstring:
Resolves imports to verify they exist and are accessible.

Caches resolution results for efficiency.

symbols.name: LateImportValidator
symbols.docstring:
Validates lazy import usage and module structure.

Checks:
- lateimport() call syntax and arguments
- Import resolution (module and object exist)
- __all__ declarations match definitions
- TYPE_CHECKING block structure
- Import organization
